keyEvents.event: Defeat in World War II and division
keyEvents.impact: Profound culture of military restraint and atonement; 'never again' as a foreign policy axiom
Security permanently anchored in the Atlantic alliance rather than national rearmament

keyEvents.event: Treaty of Rome and European integration
keyEvents.impact: National interest pursued through the EU; economic power as a substitute for military power
Became Europe's economic anchor while reassuring neighbors of its benign intentions
Reluctant hegemon of the EU; fiscal discipline as ideology

keyEvents.event: Refugee crisis and one million arrivals
keyEvents.impact: Deepened polarization and fueled the rise of the far-right AfD

keyEvents.event: Zeitenwende after Russia's invasion of Ukraine
keyEvents.impact: Historic break: 100bn euro rearmament fund, end of Russian energy dependence

history.narrative: Europe's economic engine and reluctant leader, conditioned by its past to abhor military adventurism and pursue power through trade, institutions, and consensus. The Zeitenwende is slowly forcing it to accept hard-power responsibilities it spent decades avoiding.

redLines.description: Armed attack on a NATO or EU member state
Collapse or fragmentation of the EU single market and eurozone
Russian aggression against NATO's eastern flank

internalDivisions.name: Far-right AfD surge vs centrist consensus

goals.description: Preserve EU economic leadership and export-led industrial model
Deter Russian aggression by rebuilding the Bundeswehr and arming Ukraine
Build European strategic autonomy in defense and energy
Defend multilateralism, international law, and EU cohesion
De-risk supply chains from China without losing its largest export market
